title: cephaloxys flabellata
intro: Cephaloxys flabellata, also known as the Mexican tea tree or yerba de la Florida, is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Cephaloxys flabellata include its ability to treat digestive issues, fever, and skin conditions, and it is also used as a natural remedy for wounds and sore throats. Cephaloxys flabellata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are commonly used to harness its medicinal properties.
